zh.Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯
zh.Amesbury, Wiltshire, England: 英國英格蘭維爾爾阿姆斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Baldwin Le Wak or Wake於1236年3月28日出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯時，他的父親Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire33歲，母親Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham21歲。他於1254年與Ela Beauchamp在英國諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1282年2月5日在英國波藍德享年45歲。

zh-hans.Towcester, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baldwin Le Wak or Wake于1236年3月28日出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯时，他的父亲Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire33岁，母亲Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham21岁。他于1254年与Ela Beauchamp在英国諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1282年2月5日在英国波藍德享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Wak
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from the personal name Walko.
German: occupational name, a variant of   Walke  .

English (Devon): apparently from Middle English walk ‘path, footway, place for walking’, perhaps a topographic name denoting someone who lived near or by a footpath. In some cases this may be a variant of Walkey, mainly found in Devon and Cornwall, which is unexplained.

variants.name: Walke
meanings: German: metonymic occupational name for a fuller, from Middle High German walke ‘fulling mill’. Compare Walker .
English (Devon): variant of   Walk  .

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Wake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

occupational name for ‘(man of) the watch’, from Middle English wak, wake ‘wake, vigil, watch, period of guard duty’ (Old English wacu, Old Norse vaka). It alternates with the synonymous Middle English wache (Old English wacce), hence the variant Watch. The surname was also interchangeable with Waite (the equivalent Old French term) which, like Wake, was sometimes translated as Latin vigil ‘(one who is) watchful, alert’. Compare the synonymous Vail . When the bearers had high social status, the surname probably denoted an officer with responsibility for the security of a castle, town, or royal person. Compare Wakeman .
variant of   Wack  .

variants.name: Vail
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French de la veille ‘of the watch, watchman’, with loss of de; compare Wake . There has been some confusion with the topographic name Vale and the nickname Veal .
Scottish: shortened and altered form of McPhail , Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phàil ‘son of Paul’.
Irish: variant of   Veale  .

variants.name: Waite
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a watchman, either in a town or castle, from Anglo-Norman French waite ‘watchman, member of the watch’ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Wachter ). In the Middle Ages the town waits typically combined the functions of watchmen and musicians through being trumpeters or pipers. There may also have been some late confusion with White or Wheat .

nameMeanings.name: Baldwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. In the Middle Ages it was a comparatively common name, which gave rise to a surname. It was borne by the Norman crusader Baldwin of Boulogne, who in 1100 was elected first king of Jerusalem, and by four further crusader kings of Jerusalem. It continued to be used by some families in England into the 17th century, but in modern times it normally represents a transferred use of the surname rather than a direct revival of the Norman given name.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Baldwin Le Wak or Wake was born on 28 March 1236, in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire, was 33 and his mother, Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, was 21. He married Ela Beauchamp in 1254, in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 5 February 1282, in Cumberland, England, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baldwin Le Wak or Wake est né le 28 mars 1236, à Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Ela Beauchamp en 1254, à Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 5 février 1282, à Cumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Baldwin Le Wak or Wake am 28. März 1236 in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire 33 und seine Mutter Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham 21. Er hat 1254 in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England Ela Beauchamp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 5. Februar 1282 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Cumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Baldwin Le Wak or Wake è nato il il 28 marzo 1236 nel Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Ela Beauchamp nel 1254, nel Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 5 febbraio 1282, nel Cumberland, England, all'età di 45 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Baldwin Le Wak or Wakeが1236年3月28日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリスウオルスで生まれた時，父Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshireは33歳，母Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottinghamは21歳であった。1254年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリスウオルスでEla Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1282年2月5日にイギリスクウンバーランドで45歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Baldwin Le Wak or Wake(이)가 1236년 3월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 블리스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1254년 영국 노르섬프토실르 블리스워스에서 Ela Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1282년 2월 5일 영국 쿠벌랜드에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.28 March 1236–5 February 1282: 28 de março de 1236–5 de fevereiro de 1282
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Baldwin Le Wak or Wake nasceu em 28 de março de 1236, em Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Ela Beauchamp em 1254, em Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 5 de fevereiro de 1282, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baldwin Le Wak or Wake родился 28 марта 1236 г. в местности блисворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Ela Beauchamp в 1254 г. в местности блисворт, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 5 февраля 1282 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 45 лет.

es.28 March 1236–5 February 1282: 28 de marzo de 1236–5 de febrero de 1282
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baldwin Le Wak or Wake nació el 28 de marzo de 1236, en Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Ela Beauchamp en 1254, en Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 5 de febrero de 1282, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 45 años.
